name: Moravian Sugar Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Cream the butter until smooth. Gradually add the sugar, beating well at a medium speed with an electric mixer. Add the egg and vanilla extract; beat until combined. In a separate bowl, combine 2 1/4 cups all-purpose flour, baking soda, and salt. Alternately mix in buttermilk until just combined. If the dough is sticky, add an additional 1/4 cup of flour. Cover and refrigerate for at least 8 hours.
Work with one-fourth of the dough at a time. Roll out on a lightly floured surface using a stockinette cover placed over the rolling pin. Roll to 1/16-inch thickness on a lightly floured pastry cloth. Cut with a 3-inch round cutter, and place cookies on lightly greased cookie sheets. Bake at 375°F for 6 to 8 minutes or until the edges begin to brown. Cool slightly on the sheets, then sprinkle with superfine sugar. Transfer to wire racks and allow to cool completely.
Repeat with remaining dough. Store in airtight containers. Yield: approximately 9 dozen.
Note: For old-fashioned teacakes, roll dough to 1/4-inch thickness before cutting and baking at 375°F for 8 to 10 minutes. Makes about 2 dozen.
